headline: History repeats: Mexico vs South Africa opens a World Cup again, 16 years on
summary: The 2026 World Cup opener (Mexico vs South Africa, June 11 2026, Estadio Azteca) mirrored the 2010 opener: the SAME two nations kicked off the 2010 World Cup on the SAME calendar date, June 11 2010, in Johannesburg — Africa's first World Cup. 2010 ended 1-1 (Tshabalala 55', the first goal of the 2010 tournament; Marquez 79'). RESULT 2026: history did NOT repeat the scoreline — MEXICO WON 2-0. Same fixture, same date, 16 years apart, different ending.

result_2026.scorers: Julián Quiñones 9' (the first goal of the 2026 World Cup, confirmed) and Raúl Jiménez 67' (header, assisted by Roberto Alvarado)
result_2026.cards: Three red cards (ref Wilton Sampaio) — TWO South Africa (Sphephelo Sithole; Themba Zwane 84' via VAR) and ONE Mexico (César Montes 90+2'). Count + names CONFIRMED by multiple sources (CNN/Yahoo/NBC). ⚠️ DO NOT claim a record: sources CONFLICT — some say 'first opening game EVER with three reds', others 'first in 20 years'. State the count only, never the record.
result_2026.stats: Mexico dominant — 60% possession; South Africa 0 big chances, ~0.07 xG.

angles: We said history would repeat — it DIDN'T: 2010 was a 1-1 draw, 2026 Mexico won 2-0
Same two teams, same date (June 11), 16 years apart — but a different ending
Quiñones scored the FIRST goal of the 2026 World Cup (9') — echo of Tshabalala opening 2010
Chaos at the Azteca: three red cards in the World Cup's opening game (verify count)
Raúl Jiménez's redemption header seals Mexico's home win
superlatives_to_verify: Quiñones' goal was the first goal of the 2026 tournament (it was the opening match's opening goal — likely true)
Sithole's was the FIRST red card of the 2026 tournament (FOX) — verify
THREE red cards in the opener — confirm the count; do NOT claim a 'record/most ever' without an adversarial check
